Btw, I counted some players and did some math and there is about 14,000 players. Here’s my math.
There are 9 units of players with 39 in each row and column. So 39 times itself times 9= 13,689.
There are also two units of players that have 20 in each row and column, so that is 20 squared times 2= 800
There is one unit of players that have 8 players in each row and column, so that is another 64 troops.
Last of all, fymada’s unit and the officers in netherite; fymada’s unit is 61 players including her, (5 columns of 12 players each + her) there were also about ten officers visible, but since All the officers wouldn’t have shown up since this army needs more gear,  I assumed 15, and of course we can’t forget the big three, Reina, Theo, and the biggest bird himself. So 13,689+800+64+61+15+3=14,632. Actually that is closer to 15,000. I didn’t count everyone in my initial calculations.

Also, let’s assume that cindercrest’s main army was the column of troops that were marching towards spawn. That was a column that was eight troops wide and the column didn’t end after 2 minutes. Let’s assume that that = roughly 2,000 players so if that isn’t even their main force, 5,000 max.
So it’s between 2,003 and 5,003, then find a number that is right between them, 3,503. (The 3 are saps, flame, and billy)

Yo guys heres a pattern not many people have been talking about.
The gear of army’s is becoming less quality. In the mafia arc there were thousands of players with full netherite.
In the law arc there were thousands of mostly full diamond armour and maybe 30% Netherite. In the arachnid army it’s half diamond half iron the diamonds are running out Netherite is long gone if parrot makes an army or archnid expands his army the diamonds will run out especially if people like Wemmbu kill their army’s parrot allowing 10 times more people joining unstable only makes the problem worse my the next arc or the one after 90% of people will be wearing iron armour then copper. This is a HUGE problem if it continues at this rate especially with a spoke VILLAN arc in the future and a ton of conflicts coming unless parrot does something huge the server is doomed. This is exactly what lettuce saw and why he didn’t want people out of spawn, now I disagree with him and he’s obviously evil but I see where he was coming from. Unstable is about to be very interesting.
Edit: To put it simply if you don’t want to read all my yap: Theres way way more people army’s are expanding so they need gear which is in a horrible position theres practically no netherite left diamonds are slowly becoming rarer as we see in arachnids army which is mostly half iron. So in a few arcs or maybe sooner depending on the pace of the story these huge army’s will be only wearing iron and iron Minh also become rarer which mean copper would be the only option. It might sound unbelievable that iron or copper could ever run out but there now 10 times the flow of new people joining the server who will inevitably join army’s and civilisations.

By removing them, he's allowing the "unworthy" to pass through, and clearly marking a line between the people who joined before and after (which also happend to mostly be the people who hate him vs the people who love him) And beside that, there will be problems that come with over population, parrot has done the equivalent of letting all immigrants in, which might be good in the long run, but the people who hate him wont see it that way. 
LettuceK, on the other hand, spoke directly to the people that can support him (ignoring small villages that wouldnt be worth the effort) and went after established enemies of the server. Throwing aside all morals, Parrot doesn't know how to rally people as well as Lettuce

I am convinced, at this point, that the main characters of Unstable are the 4 horsemen of the apocalypse.  Hear me out: 

Wemmbu: Death. He will take people out indiscriminately, He is not good or bad, he just is, yes he has motivations, but they are on a whim. 
Spoke: Pestilence. He is the trap virus in everyone's bases, sowing disinformation, he is the flu that not bad enough to go to the doctor about, but spreads like wildfire everywhere
Flame: War. This is a given, Flame is about combat and honor, the need to fight to prove your worth and your right to live. Also literally rides a horse. 
Parrot: Famine. No matter how much loot he finds he either walks away or disseminates it so thin amongst the populace it never makes a big enough difference, just feeds the lower rank enough not to die, but to continue starving. 

I love these stories, the plot is fantastic and its superb content. So, I, with no authority, dub you the  "Unstable horsemen". I cannot wait to see how the story progresses. My hats off to you.

Ok so after watching the whole vid, i can conclude that parrot is actually taking the exact path wifies took. Both of them taught they were doing it for the better good and were blinded by their respective objectives, wifies being protecting innocent parrot and parrot being protecting innocent players. Both of them went thru different stages of this "villain" process. First, they actually were just harmlessly chasing their objective, as a "hero", with actual wifies helping parrot and parrot helping players. Then, the middle ground, where they are slowly becoming the "villain", with bits of realisation (for example when wifies abbandoned parrot and burned the compass at the farlands vid, but later realised what he did and came back, or parrot right now revealing wemmbus identity, but then thinking if it was right or wrong). And then, they just become the "villain" with "the director" becoming this egocentric character that is blinded by his obsession of protecting parrot, which he ll do by all means, good and bad. I think the same could happen to parrot some day, maybe justified by the trauma he revevied thruought yhe whole mafia lore. This would also lowkey add up to what ash said, that parrot/spoke would become the very thing they sweared to destroy, without even realising it. This may be a stretch but it would actually make sense and would be an amazing end to the whole lore, with parrot using his intelligence to destroy the server.
Hopefully that doesnt happen tho, and i lowkey wanna see what examples he uses to show the final stage or how the series will go on now. Maybe parrot actually doesnt do the same mistake as wifies and slowly comes to a "realisation", who knows.

A villain no one talks about in the unstable universe is Parrot. I mean, this Parrot has a righteousness complex, and while it is admirable, it is twisted so much that he thinks he's the savior of the server when he's its biggest villain. Parrot had befriended an invisible mafia member, a.k.a. Deanthebean9 and then killed him while trying to "save the server" and another time when Parrot created a villain called the Director, a.k.a. Wiffes killed multiple members of B.A.T. because he wanted to "free the players and save the server" at the expense of the lives of those around him to the point his best friend Wiffes couldn't deal with it and started his villain arc to protect Parrot in his own twisted but reasonable way. Another one is Parrot defending a bandit, a.k.a. Boomie just because he thinks he's an innocent player when he's killed hundreds and stolen many items from players in the server.

But those are just the people he's defended and befriended. We still haven't talked about the things he's done in the server. He's a thief! He goes around and steals the items of many rich players for the sake of so-called "helping the server" when it does nothing for it. Acting like he isn't a criminal. He's stolen from Reddoons and Clownpierce. Who's created civilizations for the players like Capital City to help the people in spawn with gear and a home or the Clownpierce Kingdom, which annually does a Nether Festival in the Nether for the players of the server. While Parrot has done nothing for the community but give the items that aren't even his.

And that's just the tip of the iceberg; Parrot is also a massive manipulator. Tricking players like Flamefrags or the entire server that he's a man of his word when he just twists it to whatever is convenient for him to accomplish his goal. He even tricked players from Capital City that Invisible Knight, a.k.a. Wemmbu (a player who was trying to find his purpose in the server even made amends for the things he's done to other players while he was the Invisible Knight, even exposing his identity, which Wemmbu wanted to hide just to help players) committed a theft when it was he who did it. And even gaslight the player he stole from by giving it back, or Flamefrags (a player who loves fighting and just wants to rematch Wemmbu), whom Parrot promised to help but just used him for his own gain! Even giving away his stolen items like he earned them, acting like the items he's gotten aren't stolen goods when they are! Which is why I believe that Parrot is the biggest villain in the unstable universe.

P.S: This is just an idea I thought of when watching unstable videos. No hate for the content creator Parrot; just wanted to say this out there. Love Parrot's videos! Hope to watch more soon!

That "shut up" was personal😂

Theo and parrots contrasting characters are so interesting.

I’ll start with Theo. We had hints the past 2 that Theo didn’t like parrots “heroic ideas” but this one  we got into it. Theo isn’t as power hungry as Wemmbu or others but he also isn’t as morally perfect as parrot. He isn’t evil but he has his own self interests and puts himself and friends above others.
So parrots whole idea to save the server just sounds unrealistic to him and it’s like he dismisses it as some weird phase that parrot will forget about.

He is similar to wifies in the way that he is annoyed parrot values others above himself to the point where he can’t bare being with him because he is so reckless. But Theo just decides to leave instead of trying to prevent parrot like wifies did.

Before I talk about parrot I want to mention both parrot and Theo’s acting was incredible this video that scene at 30:00 was so good.

Parrot wholeheartedly believes that he can and is the only one willing enough to save the entire server. That’s all he cares about. Why? Well the scene at  33:00 with a flashback to all parrots loss explains it. He has lossed everything he has ever cared about and it only makes sense for him to be the one to stop this grief for good.

But it’s this ambition which they had a problem with. He agrees with the message but the difficulty of the task and the risk isn’t worth it to him.

51:37 the scene at the end where the meet up again is great. It’s clear their relationship isn’t fixed yet but the message from parrot is great. Even if he dosent save everyone as long as he tries his best that is what matters.

One last thing I wanted to mention to see if anyone else noticed it but so many moments I get the ashwag vibes from what parrot is saying but not in an evil mafia way. Like parrot is trying to accomplish something for a similar reason to ash except in a way that’s good for everyone.

when the invisible guy went "so you don't actually know who i am" Parrot just goes blank! like he just goes blank and remembers wifies! like what!!1 and his voice when he says "no" ahhh my heart, poor parrot

This is a great dialogue which exactly parallels wifies. This is exactly the motivation of Parrot and the reason why he do this every single time.
He wants to save everyone because he was empowered by wifies long long ago, when he was hunted by Clown. He took every single one of them on since Wifies proved that Parror CAN do it. Then Luigi and Dean died due to him pulling back instead of saving them. And then Wifies was directly countered. Parrot saved the server from Mafia with a team, he put everything on the line to save every single one and then Wifies went to such a length to prove he is risking himself.

If Parrot didn't try to save every single person up until here - he would prove that old Wifies was wrong and the Director was right all along. That Parrot is reckless and putting himself in danger for the cause in his head. That it's his fault.

The problem is - that was, but not solemnly and that's the lesson he learns from Theo and Wemmbu. None of them were there and yet Theo precisely word for word said the same thing. He is not Wifies and yet he proves Parrot wrong. This is fire

In my mind, the director has to be either Wifies of Parrot.

In real life, Parrot IS the director. He killed Wifies in the script, and he made Itz and Cube hunt him. He made everything in the script, which makes sense as to why people call him the most powerful player. He can literally control who lives and who dies by putting it in the script. The question is why? Why do all of this? Is it a test for yourself? What's the point? It's also kinda fourth wall breaking which idk if that's what they want to do.

On the other hand, EVERY death in the series ends with "<Player> left the game" except for the death of Wifies. Even 2 videos after his death, this is still true. This also does leave a few questions though. For one, what's Wifies's motive? Why try and kill someone who was your friend for so long? Why fake your death? Second, is Wifies really that powerful? We obviously know he's smart, but there's no way he'd survive a 1v1 against Itz, for example. Third, how does he seemingly know so much? In the book at the ending, how does he know Jumper game Cube a dandelion? I mean this could go for anyone, but the only way the director would know is if Cube wrote that part of the book or Jumper was on the inside. Any other way and there's no way the director could write that. That is, unless the director is Parrot after all. He could've easily written it then cut it out, as there is a cut at 1:04:58.


Those are just my theories though, let me know if there's any other evidence for who the director is.

Ngl i thought this whole video Dean was gonna snap at any point in time.

Bro had all his animals killed, constantly gets underestimated even though he's proved he has his own set of skills that just don't align with pvp or combat in general.
Never gets taken seriously to an almost unfunny degree [although it is funny sometimes] and treated as if he's dumb, and then gets devalued right next to Parrot in front of him.
Then trying to convince Parrot to leave Dean behind just so they can go, and when he doesn't agree he just starts picking on Dean out of agitation. 
Then in both scenarios besides his own, Dean _dies_ regardless.
Then he gets called a liability by the people who he'd [i'd assume] call his friends _and_ the people who saved him.
Then Wifies tries to leave Dean behind again.
Then he's told they're better off without him, just before it's revealed that he had to make an impossible decision between his life over his friends and the fear.
Then just gave up his only leverage he had freely, probably fully believing they really are better off without him.

I'm surprised he's just kept it together this entire time. I'm so sad. 
And then Parrot listening but not actually hearing or seeing what Wifies is saying from his perspective 💧 
And the most ironic thing about Parrot reading Luigi's journal entries is that he doesn't really learn anything from what Luigi went through. Like one of the entries talking about how people won't think the same as you and aren't interested in certain things you might be. People not playing the game for fun anymore, just to play. 
I don't think Parrot knows how to play for fun anymore in his journey and maybe that's why he couldn't see from Wifies' perspective, along with being a little selfish and blinded by his sense of righteousness. And dragging people into a war that isn't theirs to fight, and that they didn't ask for, probably isn't going to get him very far...

The storytelling is on point as always.

Also, if I can try to provide some constructive criticism: while this is staged, and even officially admitted to be staged, some of the staging feels a bit too apparent, and it kind of gets in the way of the immersion into the story. Just as an example: the spying at the beginning of the video doesn't really feel covert/stressful (bucket clutch would be audible, killing the creeper would be audible, you're not hiding with invis pot and taking your armor off, when they fly away they literally have you in their field of view...). Could be something to try and improve on, maybe.

The lore is crazy, we still don't know what the Wonders do, we don't know what Wemmbu and Spoke's plan are, The storytelling and timing of everything is a masterpiece. A lot of people don't like scripted SMP's but this was perfectly put together and I love this series so far. Hope to see more lore-driven content like this and can't wait to see the next episode of Unstable Universe!

Marvelous job Parrot. I know I'm 2 years late to your channel but I would like to say some stuff. I started watching you 12/24/25 Christmas Eve. I was watching you very long that night, and to this day I never regretted that decision. From when this comment was posted, yesterday is when I started watching lifesteal. I loved lifesteal, literally everything about it. I don't think anybody will ever be able to understand how much your videos mean to me. This is probably ONE OF THE BEST Minecraft videos i have ever seen. How much i can learn and how much feelings i experienced while watching this video.. sadness, amazed, nervous, anxious but it was never happy then i thought.. why was it never happy? That's when i realized, to be happy you must have peace but to achieve peace you have to be great. In order to be great you must take sacrifices even if it hurts, even if you know it will ruin your trust in relationships with them. But this is the only way you can be great by showing your true powers, by being ruthless towards your decisions. Till then no one will try to mess with you but this will only leads to loneliness, sadness and even depression.. maybe this wasn't the peace you meant... This is all because you betrayed them, stabbed them in the back while they thought you all were friends and they lost their *trust* in you. So through this i learnt that you either be the villains or the survivor but of course you wanted to be the villains to have power... But you'll definitely regret it. So i learnt that if you wanted to be powerful you will suffer from regrets but if you wanna be the survivor you will suffer from the attacks, personally i feel like ill be the survivor after seeing this video.

It's funny how you bring up returning to the Console version of minecraft. This is literally the first video I've seen of you Parrot, and a mere hour ago I did just that and found my old, old world I shared with my cousins and friends. Many of which I have no contact with anymore for quite awhile now since the days of Highschool. I roamed around the world, and it's amazing to see just how many memories we made we've forgotten about in our minds but were entirely preserved in this timeless game. Then it hit me, my friend Mikes mansion, he was the servers best build and he sadly passed due to illness a good two or so years ago. I stumbled upon it and just stared, his name was there and everything, but the front door was still wide open, before we stopped playing that door was never closed. I wanted to venture into it, to see just what he had built but opted to close the door and not disturb anything inside before going to our servers main village to see many doors weren't closed. I closed each and every one of them before taking a final glance on the village we'd built. I couldn't help but tear up to realize I'm getting older, so is minecraft but these memories we place down block by block are eternal as long as the files remain. Minecraft is that sandbox you never have to clean up and can just return to years from now and it'll be exactly how you left it. Sadly, that village shall remain frozen like I had left it after all these years, all those doors finally closed so the tamed wolves don't gotta sit with a breeze in the home. It's heart wrenching to go back and revisit worlds you made as KIDS, YOUNG TEENS while being an adult. Yet all I could do was smile with tears in my eyes knowing we had fun placing every single block of that village.

I would've probably done something a lot simpler using ender pearls. There's a trick in the game that allows you to clip through ceiling blocks using ender pearls by placing a piston under you in a 2-block space, activating it, then using one pearl into the ceiling before throwing one more to your side so you get on top of the blocks rather than suffocate in them.

To put it short, you can do things like teleport to the top of the nether using ender pearls if you're good enough at using the trick. I would have an ender pearl stasis chamber set up thousands of blocks away on the top of the nether and keep a friend there, that way you can run into the nether portal, go up to the bedrock layer, glitch through, then use that time you've bought to signal your friend on a call to open the trap door on the stasis chamber. By the time the hunters figure out the glitch and get up top, you'll be thousands of blocks away.

10:50 the chance of them guessing incorrectly the first time was 50% since, you know, you gave them the choice between a drop block and a safe block... the chance of them guessing incorrectly a second time in a row was 12.5%, and the chance of them guessing incorrectly a third time in a row was roughly 1.8%, so yeah, they had like a 1 in 55 chance to get dropped on the third guess

interestingly, if you didn't start out with a binary choice and instead had them pick one of any of the nine starting blocks, their chance of getting dropped on the third guess only goes down to about 1 in 85

Hey, if you ever want to become a youtuber know this... In quite a few cases, you need to know the YouTube algorithm. If you look at all of Parrot’s videos, you’ll notice something’s that are making him succeed...
A. His first video, wasn’t complete trash. People say that everyone’s first official video is trash, but in out cases, the first video is done decently.
B. If you look at Parrots first Minecraft smp video, it got 53,000 views which is way over his average views per video at the time. After that video, he had some other videos that got more than the usual but none of them really matched the first smp video (How to join the dream smp, is an exception to what I’m saying, it would take too long to explain why that got views). so, after trying various different video formats, parrot decided to make another smp video when his average viewership was increasing. And bam, like Tommyinnit’s mod videos, Parrot had found the perfect niche/target audience. That’s how these Minecraft smp videos on his channels are doing really well at the moment. Take notes from that lol. 
C. Parrot obviously knows a lot about YouTube. But no matter how much you learn about YouTube, you will always feel like there is more to learn.
D. If you go back to his how to join the dream smp video, listen to what he says closely. He’s not talking some random shit, he’s actually explaining some of the steps you need to take to become a YouTuber. 

(Sorry about this comment, I’m just a real sucker for learning the inner workings of YouTube. It’s my hobby, and what I spend the most time thinking about)

Ok,the enderpearl stasis chamber play was literally one of the smartest things I've ever seen someone do in a video game.

For the enderperal stasis Chamber, the peral doesn't despawn if it's not being rendered in. So if you (and all other players) were to unload the chunk by moving far enough away, and then logged off, it would still function.
It disappears if someone is in the same chunk as he ender pearl. But to make the statis work someone had to be here and he combat logged alot so yes he did use cheats.

Minecraft can be an adventurous game with almost limitless boundaries. However, the video shows a 100-day journey in one Minecraft chunk, a very confined space. In this experiment, a player and his friend survive by mining, crafting, fighting, building, and trading, turning an initially empty chunk into a thriving mini-world. They built essentials like a crafting table, a farm, a mob grinder, and later a gold farm in the Nether. Despite the challenge of surviving in such a small space, they managed to make their chunk aesthetically pleasing and resourceful, making for an interesting and unique Minecraft play-through.

I remember back in 2014/15 getting my first Minecraft account. It hits hard thinking how happy I was back then compared to how I feel with the game currently. For the last 2 months I’ve attempted to restrain myself from social media’s (apart from YouTube - idk if it counts) so that I can connect back to my friends and the game. They all seemed to diversify, me personally my laptop cannot handle any other games than Minecraft/Roblox (not in the best FPS) but I think I just got sick of playing Minecraft so much. 
(No need to read on so much I’m trynna save you the time, was in my feels so had to get it out, I’m sorry)

I miss my friends, but it seems they are all moving on; without me. I don’t wanna stop them, after all, I called them my friends for a reason. As much as it hurts me, I know deep down this day will come sooner or later, and it’s gonna hurt so much more later on. 

There’s like a one in a billion chance they’ll see this but, if they do, my memories at Phanatic and EG have brought me so much; including you guys who really saved my life from being so dark and lonely. 

I love you guys with all I’ve got - I wish the best for you; and yes I’m sorry i didn’t give a proper notice before going awol, it just hurts too much to do it. Soon enough, I’ll be back to say Goodbye properly. 😭 😭 

my heart is so broken rn. Whip, Syth, Seg, Lizzie, I3 gang 😂, Mars, Tempu, Surgie, Ping, Russ, Ice, Busteh, Saph, Manhaatenman, iLotts, Cryp, Kiyo and so many more. 

I love you all so much, I just hope for the best and one day; that by some miracle we can all be brought back together again. 


Thank You so much.

My best minecraft moments? I have lots of memories; im actually touched by them.. haha. I used to always play in creative mode. Never survival, because i was simply scared, (stupid lol.) I was entering a cave, made a little hole, and hiding, like an adorable minecraft chicken, (lol.) Next thing you know, a skeleton dropped onto my hole. I missed a block! My first exposure to 303 was around 2016. Maybe 2017. I was working with some mates to build a set for a Roleplay series, and strangely, one of the structures would always catch fire. The same one each time. I thought it was my mate trolling, and maybe at first he was, but the fires kept happening when the world was offline (it was bedrock edition so we were always playing lan. Sometimes I’d work on it at home by myself). One mate suggested Entity 303 but I was skeptical.
To this day I have no idea what was causing the fires. There was no lava or fire constantly burning near the building as far as I know, unless my friend hid a source, but I checked quite thoroughly. If I still had the world file on my machine I’d re-examine it, but it’s only on my back up drives now as I wiped the machine.

This unlocked a memory deep in my mind.

Herobrine is known for not being that bad. He appears in your world but doesn’t always have intentions of killing you. What he really wants to do is warn you about 303 and Null. They are supposedly the most powerful ones. That’s why he writes “hjälp” on signs which means help in Swedish. What he means by that is  “help, 303 and Null are dangerous” or “303 and Null are coming”

I started playing Minecraft Pocket Edition on its release date. I was really young back then. I remember being absolutely terrified off Entity 303, and not knowing anything about Herobrine. To me, Entity 303 was my Herobrine. He was who I was terrified of, not Herobrine. He was the one who I watched videos about, such as how to summon him. I always have a soft spot for the legend of Entity 303

I remember when I actually load into my world, I instantly found that entity 303, entity 505 and herobrine were fighting and spamming meassages! And then I quitted from my world and didn't played minecraft for like 20 days after that I thought I can't just sit here and be scared of them! And i log into my world again and found nothing! After that I was afraid going into forests, caves and even in my own house! Then I decided to delete the memory and the minecraft for 2 months and then I was alright! Even if now I remember, I didn't got scared! But still I have encountered herobrine, entity 303 and entity 505! In a forest begging for help, I can't remember what I did next or why were they asking me for help.

In my 8 survival world, I made a lot of progress. I was playing in the morning, it was around 10am EST.
I was seeing mysterious things like my base being destroyed, items missing, and blocks taken out of place. 
I was the only person who had access to this server, so then I was out in a cave and I could hear blocks being taken then put away. I went to see what was happening in the upper cave. But then my game crashed:(

Well I guess if they can manipulate the algorithm, they could do better than expected. Many you tubers are there unexpectedly, and are amongst the biggest successes of all time even though no one could tell. If you have no fame and then get to 5 million, then you’ve technically been lucky. Barely anyone gets there, even those with talent. McYum already has a big platform, and it’s difficult for that to disappear completely. It’s probably easier to post a video of barley nothing and gain followers rather than lose them. The real problem is when people find no reason to be subscribed to you anymore, so you end up losing subscribers.

Another problem could be if you end up posting too many bad videos, and your platform doesn’t grow. This is because then your biggest hits will be hidden among all of your rubbish, less successful videos, so they won’t succeed either, hence meaning even good content doesn’t bring you fame. That’s why Dream is successful. Almost everyone can agree that all of, or most of his videos are good quality.

If McYum keeps up a consistent standard of quality in his content, even if that standard is still below his current videos, then his platform should grow. However if it doesn’t grow enough, then the YouTube algorithm will no longer show McYum’s videos.

In conclusion,  McYum’s channel could go either way, but if it seems that he is able of creating content (which he has already proved he can do) and people actively watch it, then things will go well, even if they go slowly

I was watching nostalgia videos and I felt nostalgia. I thought this was weird since I started in 2018 and I didn’t get any of this. Little did I know that it wasn’t real.


3 months later I logged back onto my oldest world (on java, not bedrock) that I hadn’t been on since v1.13.2.
I explored the world for a bit and after about 10 minutes I felt something. It was real nostalgia. I read my book and quill that I wrote ages ago, I explored nether tunnels I had built before the nether update. I didn’t cry. I just felt so much pain omg it hurt.
I logged off the world, closed Minecraft, shut down my computer and went back to bed.

Now I know the real feeling of nostalgia I can finally relate to this.

At first, I was afraid of caves and the mobs. But up there, on those islands, I was safe. No dangers. Just me, building and exploring at my own pace. It was a place where everything felt calm, like my own little world in the sky.
